# Recipe One

 \normalsize

![Pad Thai](website/images/PadThai.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 14 oz Thai stir fry noodles
- 2 limes
- 2 tablespoons tamari (or regular soy sauce)
- 1/4 cup sweet and sour sauce
- 2 bell peppers
- 4 garlic cloves
- 1 package extra-firm tofu
- 1/4 cup roasted peanuts
- 2 eggs

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Boil Thai noodles according to package instructions. Rince under cold water and coat with oil.
1. Drain tofu, cut into bite-sized pieces, and season with salt and pepper.
1. Thinly slice peppers, mince garlic, chop peanuts.
1. Squeeze 2 tablespoons lime juice into a small bowl. Mix in tamari and sweet and sour sauce.
1. Heat oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Cook tofu until golden brown (5-10 minutes). Move to a paper towel lined plate.
1. In the same pan, add oil and cook bell peppers until tender. Add to the plate with the tofu.
1. In the same pan, add oil and garlic. Cook 1-2 minutes until browned.
1. Add noodles and sauce and cook about 3-5 minutes, until noodles absorb some sauce.
1. Move noodles to one side of the pan. Add a little oil and two eggs. Scramble the eggs until almost set, then stir them into the noodles.
1. Mix in tofu, peppers, and peanuts.
1. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve with lime wedges.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe Two

 \normalsize

![Baked Ziti](website/images/BakedZiti.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 1 2 oz can of anchovies
- 1 vidalia onion
- 5 garlic cloves
- 1/2 cup red wine (optional)
- 1 small can of tomato paste
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 large can of peeled tomatoes
- 2 tablespoons honey
- 3 tsp italian seasoning
- 1/8 tsp red pepper flakes
- 1 pound pasta (e.g., rigatoni or penne)
- 1 pound of ground beef or sausage
- 1 large container of ricotta cheese
- 1/2 cup grated parmesan cheese
- 1 egg
- 8 oz mozzarella

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Dice onion and mince garlic.
1. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a sauce pan. Add anchovies and cook until they break down.
1. Add onions and garlic to the pan and cook until soft.
1. Add red wine and simmer for 3-5 minutes (optional).
1. Add tomato paste and butter, and cook until fully combined.
1. At peeled tomatoes, stir breaking up tomatoes.
1. Once simmering, add honey, 2 tsp italian seasoning, pepper flakes and continue to stir.
1. Brown meat in a frying pan with 1 tsp italian seasoning.
1. Prepare pasta according to package instructions. Save 1 cup of pasta water in case you need to loosen the sauce.
1. Drain meat and put in a large bowl. Mix in ricotta, egg, half the mozzarella, half the parmesan, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Add in half the tomato sauce and all of the pasta.
1. Add mixture to a 9x13 glass pan. Top with remaining tomato sauce, mozzarella cheese, and parmesan.
1. Bake in the oven at 375 degrees for 35 minutes.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe Three


 \normalsize

![The Best Way to Eat Arugula (Serves 4)](website/images/ArugulaPasta.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 1 bag baby arugula (regular is fine, too)
- 1 pound pasta (e.g., bucatini or farfalle)
- 6-8 ounces of fresh or jarred pesto
- 1 lemon
- Grated parmesan cheese
- Pine nuts

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Boil and cook pasta according to box, strain but reserve 1 cup pasta water.
1. In the stock pot used to cook the pasta, add two tablespoons of olive oil and whisk in pesto over medium heat.
1. Whisk in 1/2 cup pasta water, a little more if needed to create a light sauce.
1. Remove sauce from heat and toss cooked pasta in the sauce.
1. Stir arugula in and cover until wilted.
1. Add juice from half a lemon, add more to taste.
1. Serve with parmesan cheese and pine nuts.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Breakfast Recipe

 \normalsize

![Overnight Oats](website/images/OvernightOats.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 1/4 tsp ground cinnamon
- 1/4 tsp ginger paste
- 2 tablespoons pine nuts
- 1 cups rolled oats
- 1 tablespoons maple syrup
- 4 teaspoons fig butter
- 1 plums
- 1 cups milk

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Combine milk, cinnamon, and ginger, with a pinch of salt in a 1-pint wide mouthed mason jar.
1. Whisk oats and maple syrup. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
1. Thinly slice plums.
1. Split between two 1-pint wide mouthed mason jars. Top with fig butter, plums and pine nuts.

 \normalsize
